item.title: Fathom AI Biotech
item.description:
• Summary: Fathom, formerly known as Atommap, has raised $47 million in Series A funding to advance its AI-driven drug discovery platform. The company uses graph neural networks and proprietary datasets to predict drug-target interactions and optimize lead molecules. Fathom has partnerships with several pharmaceutical companies and aims to expand its in-house pipeline. The rebranding reflects a strategic shift from mapping atomic-resolution structures to broader AI-enabled discovery capabilities.
  • MoAs: N/A (platform technology; no specific drug or MoA disclosed in the funding round announcement)
  • Sources: Endpoints News

item.title: Doctronic AI Program Controversy
item.description:
• Summary: Doctors in Utah are demanding the suspension of the Doctronic AI program, citing concerns over its implementation and potential impact on patient care. The controversy centers on the use of artificial intelligence in clinical settings, with physicians questioning its safety and efficacy.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: Endpoints News

item.title: Supreme Court Weedkiller Cancer Claims
item.description:
• Summary: The Supreme Court is set to consider cases involving claims that the weedkiller glyphosate causes cancer. These lawsuits, brought by individuals who developed non-Hodgkin lymphoma after exposure, could have significant implications for the agrochemical industry and regulatory oversight of pesticides.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: STAT News
